w:r.w:t: Bus, Hotel, Flights Teaser, Multi-Tenant Marketplace, Promoters, Dashboards, Cloudinary, and Google OAuth
Node.js + Express + MongoDB + Mongoose + EJS monolith first.
Use the current index.html page exactly as the visual base, then migrate it into EJS partials.
Use Cloudinary for all uploaded listing images, company logos, documents, blog images, and ticket assets.
Support email/password plus Google login/signup using Passport Google OAuth 2.0.
Use simple route file names: public.js, auth.js, customer.js, company.js, promoter.js, admin.js, employee.js, not public.routes.js.
Fully bookable bus and hotel services. Flights stay as teaser/read-only until provider integration.
Promoter 3%, platform 7%, company 90% when referral is valid. Platform 10%, company 90% without referral.

Classic Trip should become a polished multi-tenant travel and ticket marketplace where companies list services, customers book without friction, promoters earn through referral links, and companies can pay to appear higher through sponsored placements.
Launch first with bus booking and hotel booking because these are easier to control and validate.
Keep flights, trains, tours, car rentals, events, cargo, loyalty, and WhatsApp automation designed in the architecture but release them later.
Keep guest checkout from day one so customers can book without being forced to create an account.
Use role-based dashboards for super admin, company admin, company employee, customer, and promoter.
Use wallet and ledger records from the start so revenue, commission, refunds, and withdrawals are traceable.
The first EJS homepage must match the current index.html design exactly before extra redesign work.
All uploaded images and documents must go through Cloudinary, not local public/uploads storage in production.
Google login/signup must exist beside normal email/password signup.
Route files must use clean names like public.js, auth.js, booking.js, payment.js, and scanner.js.
Sponsored listings must always display a visible Sponsored badge.
Promoter commission stays pending until the trip, boarding, or hotel check-in flow is completed.
Suspended or unverified companies cannot publish active listings or receive new bookings.
express-session + MongoStore, bcrypt, Passport Google OAuth
Multer memory upload + Cloudinary upload service
Provider adapter pattern: mock payment first, then MTN MoMo, Airtel, Flutterwave/Paystack/DPO as needed
QR code value, PDF ticket generator, ticket lookup page
node-cron or queue later for commission release, reminders, expired bookings, promotion expiry
CSRF protection, rate limiting, validation, sanitized user content, secure cookies
This structure follows your naming preference: no public.routes.js. Route files are simple and clean, for example public.js.
5. Cloudinary Image and Document Upload Design
Cloudinary should be the central media store for all public and protected assets. Local uploads should only be used as temporary development fallback, not production storage.
Use Passport Google OAuth 2.0. Google login should create a user automatically when the email does not exist, and link the Google ID to an existing account when the email already exists.
Creates user with role customer unless a specific flow says company or promoter.
Creates user with role promoter and a promoter referral code.
Creates company admin user first, then sends them into company onboarding.
Attach googleId to existing user, then log them in.
Do not allow login; show account status message.
7. Exact index.html to EJS Migration Plan
Do not redesign the homepage first. Convert the existing index.html to EJS while preserving the same visual design, class names, layout, and interactions.
Create views/pages/home.ejs and paste the current index.html body structure into it.
Move global styles to public/css/base.css and component styles to public/css/components.css.
Move the demo JavaScript to public/js/home.js and split reusable behavior into modal.js, drawer.js, carousel.js, filters.js, and toast.js.
Create partials for site-head, site-header, hero-search, listing rails, listing cards, promo rails, blog grid, details modal, and site-footer.
Replace hardcoded cards with loops: featuredBusListings, featuredHotelListings, featuredFlightTeasers, sponsoredPlacements, latestBlogs.
Connect search form to /search and quick route pills to real query parameters.
Keep the flights section as a teaser until flight provider integration exists.
Homepage, search, filters, listing pages, sponsored placements, recommendations
Guest checkout, passenger/guest details, seat locks, room reservations, status tracking
Payment initiation, webhook validation, provider adapters, idempotency
Platform, company, promoter, customer refund balances and transactions
Referral links, click tracking, attribution, commission, withdrawals
Onboarding, verification, listings, inventory, employees, analytics
Sponsored search, homepage feature, route boost, hotel boost, flash deal
Reviews, refunds, disputes, fraud checks, support tickets, audit logs
Email now, SMS/WhatsApp later, booking reminders, payout updates
9. Roles, Permissions, and Clean Dashboard Scope
Overview metrics, company approvals, users, bookings, payments, refunds, disputes, reviews, fraud alerts, withdrawals, promotions, settings, audit logs.
Revenue, pending balance, listings, routes, schedules, seats, rooms, employees, reviews, promotions, reports, payout visibility, support messages.
Today passengers/guests, QR scanner, booking verification, check-in list, availability updates, support notes.
Upcoming bookings, past bookings, booking lookup, cancellations, refunds, saved listings, profile, loyalty placeholder.
Referral links, clicks, conversions, pending commission, available balance, withdrawals, top services, campaigns, marketing materials.
Dashboards should be clean, white-space friendly, with rounded cards, consistent font sizes, clear table filters, soft borders, simple charts, and no overloaded single-page design.
Review, RefundRequest, Notification, SupportTicket, AuditLog, BlogPost, Setting
User: role, fullName, email, phone, passwordHash, googleId, authProviders, status, isVerified, companyId, lastLoginAt
Company: ownerId, name, slug, companyType, logo, coverImage, verificationStatus, documents, supportContacts, walletId, settings
Listing: companyId, serviceType, title, slug, country, city, address, priceFrom, media[], ratingAverage, reviewCount, isSponsored, isFeatured, status
Route: listingId, origin, destination, boardingPoints, dropoffPoints, baggageRules, cancellationRules
TripSchedule: routeId, departAt, arriveAt, basePrice, currency, totalSeats, availableSeats, status
Seat: scheduleId, seatNumber, seatClass, priceDelta, status, lockedUntil
Room: listingId, roomType, capacity, nightlyPrice, inventory, amenities, media[], status
Booking: bookingRef, serviceType, guestSnapshot, customerUserId, companyId, listingId, scheduleId, passengers, pricing, promoterAttribution, paymentStatus, bookingStatus, qrCodeValue
Payment: bookingId, provider, providerReference, amount, currency, status, paidAt, rawPayload
WalletTransaction: walletId, ownerType, ownerId, transactionType, direction, amount, currency, status, referenceType, referenceId
Commission: bookingId, promoterId, companyId, platformFee, promoterAmount, companyAmount, status, releasedAt
Valid promoter attribution: promoter earns 3%, platform earns 7%, company earns 90%.
No valid promoter attribution: platform earns 10%, company earns 90%.
Commission is pending until the service is completed.
Refunded bookings cancel promoter commission and reverse unreleased company earnings.
Promoters cannot earn from their own bookings.
Email and phone are required for guest booking.
Seats or rooms must be temporarily locked during checkout.
Every paid booking receives a unique QR ticket value.
A ticket cannot be validated twice.
Scanner validation must record employee, company, time, and result.
Only verified companies can publish active listings.
Suspended companies cannot receive new bookings.
Sponsored content must always show Sponsored.
Reviews from abusive or fake accounts should go into moderation.
12. Route Map with Clean File Names
GET /, GET /search, GET /listings/:serviceType/:slug, GET /companies/:slug, GET /blogs, GET /tickets/:bookingRef
GET /login, POST /login, GET /register, POST /register, GET /auth/google, GET /auth/google/callback, POST /logout
AJAX endpoints for search, listings availability, guest booking, payment initiation, webhooks, uploads, and scanner validation
Customer searches origin, destination, and date.
Search service returns matching schedules with sorting and filters.
Customer opens listing details and selects a seat.
System creates a temporary seat lock.
Customer enters passenger and contact details.
System creates a draft booking and calculates referral split if a valid referral exists.
Webhook confirms payment and marks booking as confirmed.
QR ticket and PDF ticket are generated.
Booking becomes checked_in, then completed, then earnings are released.
Customer searches city, dates, and guest count.
Search service returns available hotel rooms.
Customer chooses room and enters guest details.
System creates a temporary room reservation.
Customer pays or reserves depending on hotel policy.
Booking completes and pending company/promoter earnings are released.
Build a provider-agnostic payment service so MTN MoMo, Airtel Money, cards, and mock payments can share one interface.
Use idempotency keys so duplicate payment callbacks do not double-credit wallets.
Create ledger entries for every money movement.
Keep company and promoter earnings pending until service completion.
Refunds must create reversing transactions rather than deleting old transaction history.
Support service type, route, city, date, price range, rating, verified status, instant confirmation, refundability, and availability filters.
Support sorting by recommended, cheapest, fastest, top rated, most booked, sponsored, and best value.
Recommended ranking should combine price competitiveness, rating, booking volume, availability, verification, and sponsored weight.
Sponsored listings can rank higher but must remain labeled Sponsored.
Prevents two customers from buying the same seat or room at the same time.
Guest customers can find tickets using booking ref + phone/email.
Builds trust before companies publish listings.
Tracks admin actions, payout approvals, refunds, and sensitive updates.
Handles bus no-show, hotel rejection, wrong seat, refund issues, and customer complaints.
Detects self-referrals, suspicious clicks, abnormal refunds, and repeated failed payments.
Gives customers and companies a formal place to resolve issues.
Companies can give promoters banners, short text, and campaign links.
Helps Classic Trip rank on search engines and explain routes/hotels/travel tips.
Helps deployment monitoring know the app is alive.
Validate required environment variables on startup.
Use centralized error handling and structured logs.
Use CSRF protection for forms and rate limiting for auth/payment endpoints.
Validate every form and API endpoint.
Use secure, httpOnly session cookies in production.
Limit upload size and file types before sending to Cloudinary.
Sanitize user-generated content like descriptions, reviews, and support messages.
Verify signed payment webhooks before marking payment as successful.
Create indexes for common search, booking, and ledger queries.
Keep daily backups and staging environment before launch.

npm install express mongoose ejs dotenv bcryptjs express-session connect-mongo cookie-parser csurf helmet express-rate-limit express-validator multer cloudinary passport passport-google-oauth20 qrcode slugify uuid nodemailer node-cron pdfkit
Foundation: package setup, src structure, env validation, MongoDB, EJS, static files, error handling
App boots, DB connects, homepage renders, assets load.
Exact homepage migration from index.html to EJS
Homepage looks the same, CSS/JS are split, seeded cards render dynamically.
Email login and Google login work; roles redirect to correct dashboards.
Company logos, covers, documents, listing images, and blog images upload and delete correctly.
Admin can approve/reject; unverified companies cannot publish.
Companies can manage bus routes, schedules, seats, hotel rooms, and availability.
Customers can search/filter real bus and hotel listings.
Guest can book, pay through mock provider, and receive confirmed booking.
Paid ticket can be validated once and marked checked in.
Revenue split records are created and pending earnings release after completion.
Promoter can create links, track clicks/bookings, and request withdrawal.
Companies can buy sponsored visibility and see campaign analytics.
Support workflows exist before public scale.
Tests, indexes, backups, monitoring, staging, deployment scripts, secrets rotation.
Unit test commission calculations, booking pricing, refund rules, promotion ranking, and availability changes.
Integration test auth/RBAC, Google login, guest booking, payment webhook, QR validation, refund request, and withdrawal approval.
End-to-end test search-to-booking-to-payment-to-ticket, employee scan flow, promoter attribution flow, and sponsored listing visibility.
Visually compare the migrated EJS homepage against the current index.html before redesigning anything.
The safest first launch is a controlled release with verified bus operators and hotels only. Add promoters and sponsored listings as beta features after the booking and ticketing flows are stable.
Launch with a small number of verified bus companies and hotels.
Use mock payment in development and one real provider in production first.
Monitor every booking, refund, and payout manually during the first weeks.
Keep flights, trains, tours, car rentals, events, loyalty, WhatsApp delivery, and mobile app as later releases.
Create the src folder structure exactly as shown in this document.
Create app.js, server.js, env.js, db.js, session.js, cloudinary.js, and passport.js.
Convert the current index.html homepage into views/pages/home.ejs without changing the design.
Split CSS and JS into public/css and public/js.
Seed demo companies, bus listings, hotel listings, flight teasers, sponsored placements, and blogs.
Build auth with email/password and Google OAuth.
Build role dashboards with clean starter pages.
Add Cloudinary upload for company and listing media.
Build company verification, bus/hotel inventory, search, guest booking, payment, QR ticketing, and promoter attribution in that order.

Classic Trip should launch as a clean bus and hotel marketplace with the existing index.html homepage design preserved, Cloudinary-powered media, Google login/signup, guest checkout, verified companies, QR ticketing, promoter commission, wallet/ledger tracking, and paid visibility for companies. The architecture above is strong enough to expand into flights, trains, tours, car rentals, events, loyalty, WhatsApp support, and a mobile app without needing a rewrite.
